content: Inside every living thing, there are tiny workers called cells that do important jobs to keep us alive. One very important job is making proteins, which help our bodies grow, heal, and stay strong. mRNA is like a special message that carries instructions from the cell’s control center to the places where proteins are made. The cell’s control center, called the nucleus, has a big book of instructions called DNA. mRNA copies just one small instruction from the DNA book and takes it outside the nucleus. Outside the nucleus, the mRNA delivers its message to tiny machines called ribosomes that read the instructions. The ribosomes follow the mRNA’s instructions to put together building blocks and make a protein. Proteins can be things like muscles to help you move or special helpers to fight germs and keep you healthy. And that’s how mRNA carries important instructions inside your cells to keep everything working just right!
